_help: The repo's delivery posture — the one file pipeline requires (schema: schema/repo.schema.json). Copy this to <repo>/pipeline.repo.json as the first act of adoption, then set every field to reality. Its `type` supersedes filesystem auto-detection; its `profile` picks the failure-cost tier the standard holds you to. Keys starting with _ are inline notes and ride through (additionalProperties). Prefer a config-presets/*.repo.json starting point over this blank when a tier fits.
_type: service | app | library | docs | infra — what this repo IS. Supersedes auto-detection.
_profile: solo | team | critical — declared failure cost. Tiers are cumulative (core ⊂ team ⊂ critical). Declare what you'll actually carry; DESC-03 compares the declaration to observed reality.
_branching_model: trunk | dev-staging-main | gitflow — drives BR-04's expected refs and the `branches` fallbacks.
_environments: Declared deploy targets. Each needs name + tier (dev | staging | prod); `gated` marks a promotion gate (ENV-04 verifies protection on the prod-tier entry). Consumed by ENV-01..05.
_iac: Optional. tool ∈ terraform | pulumi | cdk | serverless | cloudformation | bicep; state_backend names remote state. Omit the whole block if this repo ships no infrastructure as code.
_uses_feature_flags: true (or a non-empty records/flags/ dir) activates the opt-in FLAG family.
_branches: Optional tier-branch names. Unset keys fall back to branching_model conventions: trunk → default_branch; gitflow → develop; dev-staging-main → dev/staging/default_branch. Consumed by BR-04, ENV-03, HOT-02.
recovery_objective: Restore service within 30 minutes of a failed deploy via redeploy-previous.
_recovery_objective: One line stating the target recovery posture (FDRT: failure-detection-to-recovery-time). Reviewed against reality by RB-04 (sign-off).
